subtitle_zh: 从需求冲击到瓶颈接力——十个板块，一场算力大建设的同一个故事
subtitle_en: From Demand Shock to Bottleneck Relay — Ten Sectors, One Story of the AI Compute Build-Out
abstract_zh: 把这十个板块并排放好，你看到的不是十个行业，而是同一台机器的十个横截面。一场需求冲击——agentic AI 真正跑通、Anthropic 的 ARR 从 90 亿美元冲向 440 亿、编程助手市场突破 300 亿——沿供应链向上传导，让每一层几乎同时断裂：H100 租金一年涨 40%、2026 年 9 月前产能全部订满；2027 年 AI 将吃掉台积电 N3 产出的 86%；DRAM 进入「四十年一遇的短缺」、价格还要再翻一倍；美国电网排队负荷约 1 太瓦，而全国峰值只有 759GW。因为经典微缩已经失效——N3 是 50 多年来最弱的一次单位晶体管降本，SRAM 干脆停止缩小——这个产业改用先进封装、HBM、光互连与 800VDC 供电来购买性能，竞争单位也从芯片爬升到机柜、再到吉瓦级园区。下面六条主线追踪短缺如何沿链传导、利润在哪一层沉淀、为什么软件才是耐久护城河，以及中美对抗如何走向「算力被卡、机器人无人对垒」的分岔。
abstract_en: Read together, the ten sectors of this book are cross-sections of a single machine. One demand shock — agentic AI began to really work, Anthropic's ARR ran from $9B toward $44B, coding assistants passed $30B — propagated upstream until every layer broke at once: H100 rentals rose ~40% into a sold-out 2026, AI claims 86% of TSMC's N3 output by 2027, DRAM entered a 'once-in-four-decades shortage,' and a terawatt of load requests queued against a 759GW US grid peak. Because classical scaling broke — N3 is the weakest cost-per-transistor step in 50+ years and SRAM stopped shrinking — the industry now buys performance with packaging, HBM, optics and 800VDC power instead of lithography, and the unit of competition climbed from chip to rack to gigawatt campus. The six through-lines below trace how the shortage travels, where the margin pools, why software is the durable moat, and how the US-China conflict bifurcates.

throughlines.name_en: One demand shock, one synchronized shortage across the whole chain
throughlines.name_zh: 一场需求冲击，一次全链条同步短缺
throughlines.body_en: The proximate cause is a repricing of tokens: agentic AI works, so coding is now >70% of OpenAI's and Anthropic's ARR, Claude Code authors ~4% of GitHub public commits, and Anthropic's inference gross margin swung from 38% to above 70%. That demand hit a chain with no slack anywhere. H100 one-year rentals rose ~40% ($1.70→$2.35/hr) with capacity booked through September 2026; every merchant and custom accelerator converges on TSMC N3 in 2026, pushing effective utilization past 100% and AI's share of N3 output to 86% by 2027; DRAM — whose density now doubles per decade, not every 18 months — entered a 'once-in-four-decades shortage' with prices set to double again; and ~1 terawatt of load requests queues against a 759GW US grid peak. Nothing responds quickly: every layer's supply lead time is measured in years, and gas-turbine makers, scarred by past busts, refuse to expand.
throughlines.body_zh: 直接起因是 token 被重新定价：agentic AI 跑通之后，编程占了 OpenAI 与 Anthropic 超过 70% 的 ARR，Claude Code 已写下 GitHub 公开提交的约 4%，Anthropic 推理毛利率从 38% 摆到 70% 以上。这股需求撞上一条毫无冗余的供应链：H100 一年期租金从 1.70 涨到 2.35 美元/小时（+40%），2026 年 9 月前的产能全部订满；所有商用与自研加速器 2026 年齐聚台积电 N3，有效利用率被逼过 100%，2027 年 AI 将占 N3 产出的 86%；DRAM 密度如今十年才翻一倍（黄金年代是 18 个月一倍），这轮短缺因此被称为「四十年一遇」、价格还要再翻倍；而约 1 太瓦的负荷申请排在 759GW 的美国电网峰值面前。链上没有任何一层能快速响应——每层的供给周期都以年计，连燃气轮机厂商都因历史上的暴涨暴跌而拒绝扩产。

throughlines.name_en: Moore's Law broke — packaging, memory and process tricks are the repair crew
throughlines.name_zh: 摩尔定律断裂——封装、存储与工艺技巧充当修复队
throughlines.body_en: The economic engine beneath everything — cheaper transistors every node — has stalled: N3 delivered ~15% cost-per-transistor improvement, the weakest in 50+ years, and the SRAM bitcell is identical across N5, N3E and Intel 18A (0.021µm²). IO is worse — data rates double every four years against transistors' two — so designs hit a pad-limited wall. Value migrates to whatever repairs the gap. TSMC's CoWoS revenue reached $9.6B, 2.5× Apple's InFO; hybrid bonding, 'more transformative than EUV,' pulls packaging into the fab because its cleanliness physics (a 1µm particle makes a 10mm bond void; ISO-3 cleanrooms) structurally lock OSATs out. In equipment, lithography is no longer destiny: High-NA single-patterning costs more than low-NA double-patterning through 1nm, so etch/deposition-led vertical scaling (3D NAND: +30%/yr density) and tricks like Sculpta and DSA carry the roadmap. Memory's repair is HBM — already 60%+ of a Blackwell GPU's manufacturing cost.
throughlines.body_zh: 支撑一切的经济引擎——每一代更便宜的晶体管——已经熄火：N3 的单位晶体管降本只有约 15%，是 50 多年来最弱的一代；SRAM 位元胞在 N5、N3E 和 Intel 18A 上一模一样（0.021µm²）。IO 更糟：晶体管密度两年翻倍、IO 速率四年才翻倍，设计撞上「焊盘受限」之墙。于是价值向修补者迁移：台积电 CoWoS 收入达 96 亿美元、是苹果 InFO 的 2.5 倍；被称为「比 EUV 更具颠覆性」的混合键合，因其洁净度物理——1µm 颗粒即造成 10mm 键合空洞、必须 ISO 3 级洁净室——把 OSAT 结构性锁在门外，将封装收进晶圆厂。设备侧，光刻不再是天命：High-NA 单次曝光直到 1nm 都比低 NA 双重曝光更贵，路线图改由刻蚀/沉积驱动的垂直堆叠（3D NAND 密度年增 30%）和 Sculpta、DSA 这类图形化技巧扛起。存储的修复方案是 HBM——已占 Blackwell GPU 制造成本的 60% 以上。

throughlines.name_en: The unit of competition climbed from chip to rack to gigawatt campus
throughlines.name_zh: 竞争单位从芯片爬升到机柜，再到吉瓦级园区
throughlines.body_en: 'Systems beat dies' is now the organizing law of the whole chain. Nvidia's 72-GPU NVLink domain is why the 8-GPU MI355X 'cannot compete head on' in frontier MoE inference; Huawei's CloudMatrix 384 answers with 16 racks, 6,912 LPO transceivers and zero copper — twice GB200 NVL72's compute at 4.1× the power, a rational trade where silicon, not electricity, is scarce. Rack physics then rewrites everything below: copper ends at ~2m reach and a 448G SerDes ceiling, pulling optics into the package (TSMC's COUPE won over even CPO-pioneer Broadcom); 600kW racks break 54V distribution, forcing 800VDC (~15× less current) and liquid cooling; the failure domain grows from node to whole rack. Finally the datacenter itself became the product: at $10-13M revenue per MW-year, xAI's 122-day, gas-turbine-powered Colossus proved that speed-to-power — not just silicon — is the moat.
throughlines.body_zh: 「好系统胜过好裸片」如今是整条链的组织法则。英伟达 72 卡 NVLink 域让 8 卡的 MI355X 在前沿 MoE 推理上「无法正面竞争」；华为 CloudMatrix 384 用 16 个机柜、6,912 只 LPO 光模块、零铜缆作答——算力两倍于 GB200 NVL72、代价是 4.1 倍功耗，在缺硅不缺电的中国这是理性交换。机柜的物理规律随即改写下游一切：铜在约 2 米互连距离与 448G SerDes 天花板上走到头，光被拉进封装（台积电 COUPE 连 CPO 先驱博通都收入麾下）；600kW 机柜击穿 54V 配电，逼出 800VDC（电流降约 15 倍）与液冷；故障域从单节点扩大到整柜。最终数据中心本身成了产品：在每 MW 每年 1,000-1,300 万美元收入之下，xAI 用 122 天、靠租来的燃气轮机建成 Colossus，证明「拿到电的速度」而不只是硅片，才是护城河。

throughlines.name_en: The value-capture war: deliberate under-pricing below, violent rotation to the labs above
throughlines.name_zh: 价值捕获之战：上游刻意压价，利润池猛烈转向实验室
throughlines.body_en: Who keeps the margin is a policy choice, not an accident. Nvidia (~75% gross margin, ~4× markup) defends share with equity checks instead of price cuts, and together with TSMC under-prices into scarcity like a 'central bank of AI,' keeping downstream labs profitable so total demand compounds. Custom silicon disciplines from below: OpenAI extracted ~30% off its entire Nvidia fleet before deploying a single TPU; Anthropic committed to ≥1M TPUs (~$10B of Broadcom racks plus ~$42B of GCP RPO); Broadcom became the silent #2 AI-chip company. HBM transfers wealth upstream to SK Hynix at 3×+ per-GB premiums. Then in December 2025 the pool snapped downstream: Anthropic's inference margin crossed 70%, Bedrock's seller-of-record structure lifted AWS EBIT +213bp while peers' margins fell, and Microsoft's capacity pause gifted Oracle ~$300B of OpenAI contracts (~$150B of gross profit; the >$420B headline is Oracle's total RPO across all customers). Every layer's price is now strategy.
throughlines.body_zh: 利润归谁是策略选择，不是市场偶然。英伟达（约 75% 毛利、约 4 倍加价）用股权投资而非降价守住份额，并与台积电一道在极度稀缺中刻意不涨价，像「AI 的央行」一样让下游实验室保持盈利、令总需求持续复利。自研芯片从下方施加纪律：OpenAI 一颗 TPU 都还没部署，就靠竞争威胁把整个英伟达机队砍价约 30%；Anthropic 承诺超过 100 万颗 TPU（约 100 亿美元博通机柜外加约 420 亿美元 GCP 订单）；博通成了沉默的 AI 芯片第二名。HBM 则把财富向上游的 SK 海力士转移——每 GB 溢价 3 倍以上。到 2025 年 12 月，利润池骤然甩向下游：Anthropic 推理毛利率越过 70%，Bedrock 的「卖方记账+双重抽成」结构让 AWS 利润率逆势 +213bp，而微软的扩产暂停把约 3,000 亿美元的 OpenAI 合同（约 1,500 亿毛利；超 4,200 亿是甲骨文面向所有客户的总 RPO）拱手送给甲骨文。每一层的定价如今都是战略行为。

throughlines.name_en: Software is the durable moat — CUDA below the API, data and harnesses above it
throughlines.name_zh: 软件才是耐久护城河——API 之下是 CUDA，之上是数据与 harness
throughlines.body_en: Hardware gaps close in a product cycle; software gaps compound. Below the API line: MI300X's paper superiority evaporated in out-of-box PyTorch; H100 training MFU rose 34%→54% in twelve months from CUDA kernels alone; one software stack (wideEP + disaggregated serving + MTP) 14×'d DeepSeek throughput on the same B300 silicon — while AMD needed 26 days of hand-written kernels to lift MI355X's Day-0 numbers >100×, the ecosystem-maturity gap made visible. Above the line, RL made data the moat: cloned-UI environments run ~$20k each and OpenAI bought hundreds; Surge passed ~$1B ARR selling expert rubrics. Token economics became harness engineering — Opus's true blended agentic price is ~$0.99/MTok against a $5/$25 sticker, because 300:1 input ratios and 90%+ cache hits set cost-per-task. Even cluster quality is software-rated: ClusterMAX steered ~$400B of contracts, and CoreWeave's SUNK scheduler keeps it the sole Platinum cloud.
throughlines.body_zh: 硬件差距一个产品周期就能追平，软件差距却在复利。API 之下：MI300X 的纸面优势在开箱即用的 PyTorch 里蒸发；H100 训练 MFU 十二个月内仅靠 CUDA 内核就从 34% 提到 54%；同一块 B300 上，wideEP+分离式推理+MTP 的软件栈把 DeepSeek 吞吐做到 14 倍——而 AMD 要用 26 天手写内核才把 MI355X 的 Day-0 成绩拉高 100 倍以上，生态成熟度差距一览无余。API 之上：RL 把数据变成护城河——克隆一个网站 UI 环境约 2 万美元、OpenAI 买了几百个，Surge 靠专家 rubric 做到约 10 亿美元 ARR。token 经济学变成 harness 工程——Opus 名义价 $5/$25，agentic 混合真实价却约 $0.99/MTok，因为 300:1 的输入输出比与 90% 以上的缓存命中率决定的是每任务成本。连集群质量都被软件评级：ClusterMAX 引导了约 4,000 亿美元合同，CoreWeave 凭 SUNK 调度器独占 Platinum。

throughlines.name_en: China's bifurcation: choked at the HBM node, unopposed in robotics
throughlines.name_zh: 中国的分岔：被卡死在 HBM，在机器人上无人对垒
throughlines.body_en: Export controls work and leak at once — and the binding constraint is memory, not logic. SMIC can print millions of Ascend dies (N+3 reaches TSMC N6-class density without EUV; its 32.5nm metal pitch is tighter than shipping Intel 18A), but China's ~13M foreign HBM stacks — 11.4M from Samsung, 7M shipped in the one-month pre-enforcement gap — are running out, and CXMT's ~2M stacks in 2026 support only 250-300k 910Cs. So Huawei builds fewer Ascends next year, even as shell fabs and 2.9M smuggled TSMC dies prove the entity list structurally defeatable. China compensates systemically: power-rich, it trades watts for wafers (CloudMatrix 384), while CXMT rationally harvests the DRAM cycle (~$8.6B revenue) instead of low-yield HBM. And on the one front with no US answer, Unitree's $27.3K humanoid carries 67% gross margin on a ~$9K BoM, and Chinese firms hold ~50% of the world's largest robot market.
throughlines.body_zh: 出口管制同时在生效与漏水——而真正卡死中国的是存储，不是逻辑。SMIC 印得出数百万颗 Ascend 裸片（N+3 无 EUV 也做到台积电 N6 级密度，32.5nm 金属间距甚至比在售的 Intel 18A 更紧），但中国约 1,300 万颗进口 HBM——三星占 1,140 万、其中 700 万是在管制公布到执行的一个月空窗里发货——正在耗尽，CXMT 2026 年约 200 万颗堆栈只够 25-30 万颗 910C。于是华为明年造的 Ascend 反而更少；而壳公司晶圆厂与 290 万颗走私台积电裸片又证明实体清单在结构上可被击穿。中国的补偿是系统性的：电力富余，就用瓦特换晶圆（CloudMatrix 384）；CXMT 理性地先收割 DRAM 周期（约 86 亿美元收入）而非死磕低良率 HBM。而在美国没有答案的战线上：Unitree 人形机 2.73 万美元定价对应约 9 千美元 BoM、67% 毛利，中国厂商已拿下全球最大机器人市场的约 50%。
